---
number-sections: false
---

<!-- See the note in data-code.qmd: .unnumbered H1, and no YAML subtitle. -->

# About {.unnumbered}

*Author & project.*

```{=html}
<div class="hm-about-card">
  <div class="avatar">photo</div>
  <div>
    <div class="name">Christina Muedsam</div>
    <div class="role">Plant virology · computational biology</div>
    <div class="links">
      <a href="https://github.com/MintyHybrid" rel="noopener">GitHub ↗</a>
      <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0001-8488-3442" rel="noopener">ORCID ↗</a>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Placeholders to fill in.** The bio line, the photo, and any further profile
links (Scholar, Bluesky, LinkedIn) are yours to write — I've left the slots
rather than inventing them. Drop an image at `images/author.jpg` and replace
the `avatar` div in `about.qmd` with
`<img src="images/author.jpg" alt="Christina Muedsam">` when you have one.
:::

## The project

This compendium follows a single thread of reasoning: a catalytic-looking
15-nucleotide sequence that turned up in several potyvirus genomes, and what
chasing it implies about horizontal sequence transfer and the host protein
sorting machinery.

It is a **preprint and work in progress**, not peer-reviewed work. Several of
its central ideas — DNAzyme activity, horizontal gene transfer, and the
AP-4 / tyrosine-motif immune hypothesis — are stated as explicit, testable
hypotheses rather than results, and are flagged as such throughout. Analyses,
figures and conclusions may change.

Constructive feedback is welcome from anyone, regardless of background. The
repository's [issue tracker](https://github.com/MintyHybrid/project-hail-mary/issues)
is the best place for it.

## Acknowledgements

Public data providers whose resources this work depends on:

- **NCBI** — sequence records and BLAST
- **GBIF** — species occurrence records
- **GloBI** — biotic interaction records
- **ELM** — the eukaryotic linear motif resource

...alongside the open-source R and Python communities behind every tool listed
in [Data & Code](data-code.qmd).

Typefaces: **Spectral**, **Michroma**, **Spline Sans** and **IBM Plex Mono**, all
under the [SIL Open Font License 1.1](https://scripts.sil.org/OFL). They are
self-hosted rather than loaded from a CDN, so reading this book sends no request
to a third party; the licence texts ship alongside the font files in
`assets/fonts/`, and `scripts/fetch_fonts.sh` regenerates both.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
Funding, institutional affiliation and individual acknowledgements are left
blank deliberately — please add whatever is accurate before this is published.
:::

## Citing this work

Until there is a preprint DOI, cite the repository directly, noting the commit
or render date. The `potytools` package carries its own citation metadata in
`packages/potytools/CITATION.cff`.

Author metadata: Christina Muedsam, ORCID
[0000-0001-8488-3442](https://orcid.org/0000-0001-8488-3442).

## Licensing

Code in `packages/potytools/` is MIT-licensed. Sequence data and other
third-party inputs under `data/` retain their original source licensing — see
the [data sources appendix](notebooks/A1_data_sources.qmd) for provenance.

```{=html}
<div class="hm-downloads" style="margin-top:2rem;">
  <a class="hm-btn accent" href="https://github.com/MintyHybrid/project-hail-mary" rel="noopener">↗ repository</a>
  <a class="hm-btn" href="data-code.html">→ data &amp; code</a>
</div>
```

<!--
Omitted from the design prototype on purpose: the password-gated "full CV"
download. The prototype implemented it with a client-side prompt() that
accepted any input and showed an alert -- it looked like an access control
without being one. A real gated download needs a server (or simply an
email address); shipping the fake version would imply protection that a
static GitHub Pages site cannot provide. A public CV link can go in the
profile card above whenever you'd like one.
-->
